query: SELECT id, name, region, description FROM atolls ORDER BY name;
command: mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway03.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 3FFv3TtDUeNbYDM.root --database JUCCYykxYWxAcNny3wop55 --execute SELECT id, name, region, description FROM atolls ORDER BY name;

rows.name: Alif Alif Atoll
rows.description: Alif Alif Atoll is part of Kaafu and features spectacular coral reefs and world-class dive sites including famous shipwrecks. Home to vibrant marine life including manta rays, whale sharks, and sea turtles, this atoll is perfect for divers and snorkelers seeking pristine underwater experiences.

rows.name: Alif Dhaal Atoll
rows.description: Alif Dhaal Atoll offers a more tranquil experience compared to its northern neighbor. Known for world-class diving and snorkeling, this atoll features striking underwater topography and serene vibes. Guraidhoo Island is famous for traditional Maldivian handicrafts and local souvenirs.

rows.name: Baa Atoll
rows.description: Baa Atoll is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve renowned for its amazingly rich marine life and stunning coral reefs. The atoll is home to Hanifaru Bay, a protected marine area that attracts hundreds of manta rays and whale sharks annually. Witness these majestic creatures at the world's largest natural manta ray feeding station.

rows.name: Dhaalu Atoll
rows.description: Dhaalu Atoll remains pristine and low on the dive-tourist radar, offering uncrowded sites and a sense of discovery. Features around five channels on its western edge and ten on the eastern side, each funneling nutrient-rich currents that support vibrant, healthy reefs and major pelagic action. Home to the marine protected Fushi Kandu.

rows.name: Faafu Atoll
rows.description: Faafu Atoll is a frontier diving destination with recently discovered, unspoiled sites. Its reefs offer unparalleled excitement and a genuine sense of pioneering adventure. Expect healthy reef ecosystems and major species encounters in wide channels. Perfect for experienced divers seeking frontier exploration.

rows.name: Gaafu Alif Atoll
rows.description: Gaafu Alif Atoll is the northern half of the world's largest natural atoll. A diver's dream destination, it features pristine reefs, diverse marine life, and world-class diving sites. Ideal for experienced divers seeking pristine underwater adventures in the southern Maldives.

rows.name: Gaafu Dhaalu Atoll
rows.description: Gaafu Dhaalu Atoll is the southern half of the world's largest natural atoll. Features pristine reefs and excellent diving opportunities. This remote southern atoll offers authentic Maldivian diving experiences away from crowded tourist areas.

rows.name: Gnaviyani Atoll
rows.description: Gnaviyani Atoll is the smallest of all Maldives atolls, offering an intimate and exclusive island experience. Despite its small size, it features beautiful beaches, turquoise lagoons, and excellent diving and snorkeling opportunities.

rows.name: Haa Alif Atoll
rows.description: Haa Alif Atoll (Thiladhunmathi) is the northernmost group of islands in the Maldives, opened to foreign tourism only in 2005. The lush tropical landscape is completely untouched with many uninhabited islands, reefs, and isolated white sand beaches. Home to luxury resorts and world-class diving in a remote, exclusive setting.

rows.name: Haa Dhaalu Atoll
rows.description: Haa Dhaalu is one of the most beautiful atolls in the Maldives, a tropical sanctuary with long beaches and turquoise lagoons. Perfect for those seeking a balance between relaxation and adventure, with excellent diving, snorkeling, and local island experiences.

rows.name: Kaafu Atoll
rows.description: Kaafu Atoll is home to Malé, the Maldives' capital, making it easily accessible for visitors. Boasting spectacular reefs and shipwreck dive sites, this atoll is popular among divers. Colorful coral reefs are abundant with vibrant marine life, including manta rays, whale sharks, and turtles.

rows.name: Laamu Atoll
rows.description: Laamu Atoll is less developed and known for its natural beauty and pristine landscapes. It's a top spot for surfing with consistent waves and excellent conditions. Perfect for surfers, nature lovers, and those seeking an off-the-beaten-path Maldivian experience.

rows.name: Meemu Atoll
rows.description: Meemu Atoll offers authentic local island experiences with traditional Maldivian culture. The atoll features beautiful beaches, excellent diving and snorkeling, and a more local, less touristy atmosphere compared to resort-heavy atolls.

rows.name: Noonu Atoll
rows.description: Noonu Atoll is a hot spot for the newest luxury phenomena, home to some of the most luxurious resorts in the Maldives. Features world-class diving, pristine beaches, and cutting-edge resort experiences. Perfect for luxury travelers seeking the latest in island resort innovation.

rows.name: Raa Atoll
rows.description: Raa Atoll is hugely popular due to its pristine beaches and sparkling turquoise waters. Home to some of the Maldives' most exclusive resorts, this atoll is famous for its thriving marine life and spectacular lagoons. Ideal for luxury travelers, divers, and marine enthusiasts.

rows.name: Seenu Atoll
rows.description: Seenu Atoll (Addu Atoll) is located in the southern Maldives and offers excellent snorkeling and diving opportunities. Features beautiful coral reefs, diverse marine life, and a more local, less developed atmosphere.

rows.name: Shaviyani Atoll
rows.description: Shaviyani Atoll is the least developed of all atolls in the Maldives, offering an exquisite personal hideaway with fine lagoons surrounding paradise islands. Perfect for privacy seekers, divers, and those wanting an authentic, untouched Maldivian experience.

rows.name: Thaa Atoll
rows.description: Thaa Atoll is a central atoll offering excellent diving and snorkeling opportunities. Features beautiful coral reefs, diverse marine life, and a good balance between accessibility and pristine natural beauty.

rows.name: Vaavu Atoll
rows.description: Vaavu Atoll offers excellent diving and snorkeling from local islands including Keyodhoo, Fulidhoo, Thinadhoo, and Alimatha. Features pristine reefs, diverse marine life, and authentic local island experiences in the central Maldives.
